question_text: A radio wave has a wavelength of 3 metres. Explain what this measurement tells you about the wave.
correct_answer: The distance between one wave crest and the next crest (or from one trough to the next trough) is 3 metres. This is one complete wavelength.
explanation: Wavelength is the distance occupied by one complete wave cycle. It can be measured from crest to crest, trough to trough, or any corresponding point on adjacent waves. A wavelength of 3 metres means the pattern repeats every 3 metres along the direction of travel.
